title: PDPA Risk Assessment Checklist
description: A checklist of 18 items for conducting a data protection risk assessment aligned to the Malaysia PDPA 2010. Use this checklist to ensure comprehensive coverage during each risk assessment cycle.

items.description: Confirm that the Records of Processing Activities (ROPA) under s138 is complete, accurate, and up to date. Verify that all personal data processing activities, data categories, data flows, and storage locations are documented.
evidenceRequired: System inventory with personal data flags

items.description: Assess all consent collection mechanisms across channels (web, mobile, physical, telephone) to ensure they comply with s6 (General Principle) and s7 (Notice and Choice Principle). Verify that notices are written, provided before or at the time of collection, and cover all required elements.
evidenceRequired: Consent forms and scripts across all channels
Privacy notices displayed at collection points

items.description: Review all data processing agreements with third-party processors to ensure they include adequate PDPA-compliant security measures (s9), breach notification obligations (s143A), sub-processor restrictions, and audit rights.

Verify that all personal data transfers outside Malaysia have a lawful transfer mechanism under s129A (adequacy decision, standard contractual clauses, binding corporate rules, consent, or certification). Confirm transfer impact assessments have been completed.
evidenceRequired: Executed standard contractual clauses or BCR documentation

items.description: Test the end-to-end process for receiving, verifying, processing, and responding to data subject requests (access, correction, erasure, portability, withdrawal of consent) within statutory timelines (21 days for access under s31).
evidenceRequired: Request tracking logs with response times

items.description: Assess the adequacy of technical security measures under s9 (Security Principle) and the Personal Data Protection Standards 2015, including access controls, encryption, network security, vulnerability management, and endpoint protection.

Verify that the incident response plan includes procedures for meeting the 72-hour Commissioner notification requirement under s143A, data subject notification processes, and that breach response teams are trained and available.
evidenceRequired: Incident response plan with breach notification procedures

items.description: Verify that personal data retention periods are defined for all data categories, that data is not retained beyond the defined periods, and that secure destruction methods are applied when data reaches end of life under s10 (Retention Principle).

Evaluate the effectiveness of data protection training and awareness programmes. Verify that all staff who handle personal data have completed training, and that training content covers current PDPA requirements including the 2024 amendments.

Identify all processing of sensitive personal data (health, political opinion, religious belief, commission of offence, etc.) and verify that explicit consent has been obtained and that enhanced security measures are in place.
evidenceRequired: DPIA reports for sensitive data processing

items.checkItem: Verify DPIA completion for high-risk processing
items.description: Confirm that Data Protection Impact Assessments have been completed for all high-risk processing activities as required by s137, and that identified risks have been treated with documented mitigation measures.
evidenceRequired: Risk treatment plans arising from DPIAs

items.description: Verify that a Data Protection Officer has been appointed as required under s129B (for prescribed classes of data users), that the DPO has adequate resources and authority, and that the DPO's responsibilities are clearly defined.
evidenceRequired: DPO role description and reporting line

items.checkItem: Assess data accuracy and completeness controls
items.description: Evaluate controls ensuring personal data is accurate, complete, not misleading, and kept up to date as required by the Data Integrity Principle (s11). Review processes for correcting inaccurate data.
evidenceRequired: Correction request logs and completion records

items.checkItem: Review disclosure controls and third-party sharing
items.description: Verify that personal data is not disclosed for purposes other than those for which it was collected (s8 Disclosure Principle), and that all disclosure arrangements are documented with appropriate legal basis.

items.checkItem: Verify data user registration with JPDP
items.description: Confirm that the organization has registered with JPDP as required for its class of data users under s14-s16 and the Personal Data Protection (Class of Data Users) Order 2013, and that registration details are current.
Review all direct marketing activities to ensure valid consent was obtained before sending marketing communications, opt-out mechanisms are functioning, and opt-out requests are actioned promptly.
evidenceRequired: Sample marketing communications with opt-out mechanism

items.description: Assess the overall data protection governance framework including policies, procedures, organizational structure, board oversight, risk management integration, and accountability mechanisms.
evidenceRequired: Internal audit reports on PDPA compliance

items.checkItem: Review sector-specific code of practice compliance
items.description: If the organization falls under a registered class of data users, verify compliance with the applicable sector-specific code of practice (banking, healthcare, communications, insurance, aviation, utilities) in addition to the General Code of Practice 2022.
evidenceRequired: Applicable code of practice compliance assessment
